steps.title: Identify the main clause verb requirement
steps.content: The sentence is missing its main verb. The subject “Puerto Rico's museums” must pair with a verb that makes a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content: A main clause requires a finite verb—a verb form showing tense that stands alone. Choice D “inform” is the simple present tense form for a plural subject. Choices A, B, and C provide non-finite forms—a past participial phrase, a present participle, and an infinitive—that need auxiliary verbs. That matches choice D — D is correct.
